name: Bombling_Mech

infobox.Systems: Bomb Dispenser(Ranged)EffectLaunch a Walking Bomb. Unused bombs dismantle after the enemy turn.Upgrade I2 Bombs ()Fire a second Walking Bomb in a different direction.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theBombermechssquad.
sections.Strategy:
Compared to other Ranged-class Mechs, the Bombling Mech deploys low-damage temporary units without any pushing utility. Although this makes it tough to fit in aCustom Squad, the Bomblings it deploys bring other forms of utility to the Bombermechs squad; both thePierce Mechand theExchange Mechhave specific positional requirements, which the Bomblings can satisfy by being pierce and swap targets. By placing a Bombling unit directly in useful location, the player can utilize its position with one of the other mechs prior to moving it, serving multiple purposes during a single turn.
Bomblings are also ideal throwaway units for intercepting projectiles or blockingspawns, much like the rock launched by theBoulder Mech. While they only deal 1 damage when attacking by self destructing, a Bombling can also garner additional value from ignitingforests, stirring upsandwith smoke, and breakingice tilesorcracked tiles. If Bomblings can't safely detonate, they can also be used for knock damage from the Pierce Mech's push.
Because Bomblings have their own movement of 3 tiles, the Bombling Mech itself is often flexible in terms of positioning, itself also being used to satisfy positional requirements for the other mechs, or to block shots or spawns. Note, however that the bomblings movement cannot be upgraded.
While it costs 3 cores, the2 Bombsupgrade to launch a second bombling is strong, as it grants an additional action during the turn. Note that this second bombling must be launched in a different direction than the first. Alternatively, a poweredSilicacan be used to deploy two bomblings in a single turn, only costing 2 Reactor Cores for his ability instead of 3. When both his abilityandthe upgrade are powered, he can launch four Bomblings in a single turn. In general, though, the Bombling Mech does not have heavy pilot requirements, and the squad's best pilots can be used elsewhere.
Boostability does not work on a bombling.
Despite all the utility that Bomblings offer, picking up a more conventional rangedweaponis still recommended, as it can offer important flexibility later in the run. Preferably a ranged projectile that can push enemies away, for when launching a bombling will not suffice to neutralize a threat.
